steps.text: Identify the two cost components: a daily rate of \$11 per day, and a one-time insurance fee of \$10. The total cost \(c\) for \(d\) days is \(c = 11d + 10\).
Check with \(d = 1\): \(c = 11(1) + 10 = 21\). One day of rental costs \$11, plus \$10 insurance = \$21. ✓
Check with \(d = 3\): \(c = 11(3) + 10 = 43\). Three days at \$11 each = \$33, plus \$10 insurance = \$43. ✓

Evaluate each choice:
  • A: \(c = 11(d+10)\) — expands to \(11d + 110\), charging \$110 for zero days instead of \$10. Eliminated.
  • B: \(c = 10(d+11)\) — expands to \(10d + 110\), uses the wrong daily rate (\$10 instead of \$11) and wrong fixed fee (\$110). Eliminated.
  • C: \(c = 11d + 10\) — daily charge \(11d\) plus one-time fee \$10. Matches the problem exactly. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: \(c = 10d + 11\) — swaps the daily rate and the fixed fee; charges \$10/day and a \$11 one-time fee. Eliminated.
